title: Crime Revenue vs. Penalty Cost
query: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(TotalReward), 0) AS earned FROM Bounty WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(Amount), 0) AS penalties FROM PayBounty WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate UNION ALL SELECT COALESCE(SUM(Amount), 0) FROM PayFines WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate

details.text: {earned} Cr earned from bounty activity against a penalty spend during the report period. Did crime actually pay? This comparison answers it directly — and might reveal that your law-breaking career is costing significantly more than it generates.
details.help: earned is bounty rewards; penalties subquery sums PayBounty + PayFines expenditure. earned drives the title as the gross crime revenue.

tagLines: Crime profitability analysis compiled. Gross criminal income vs. total legal remediation costs noted.
The math is honest. Whether the result is flattering is between you and your accountant.
Criminal careers are subject to market forces. The penalty column is the overhead no one talks about.
